image_prompt: SVG: velocity-time graph of a migrating bird. x-axis Time / s (0 to 600, ticks every 100), y-axis Velocity / m s⁻¹ (0 to 35, ticks every 5). Trapezoidal motion through vertices A(0,0), B(100,30), C(450,30), D(550,0); teal line with × markers, vertices labelled A B C D.

SVG: four oscilloscope sine traces A, B, C, D stacked, common x-axis Time / s (0 to 1, gridlines at 0, 0.5, 1). Cycle counts in 1 s: A=10 Hz, B=12 Hz, C=6 Hz (largest amplitude), D=24 Hz (densest). Teal traces on white, panel letters A-D.

render_data.title: Sound waves recorded from four sources A, B, C, D (1 second window)
render_data.concept: Reading frequency from an oscilloscope display: frequency = complete cycles per second (Hz).
render_data.task: Count cycles in 1 s to find each frequency (A=10 Hz, B=12 Hz, C=6 Hz, D=24 Hz). v=fλ with v=330 m/s gives wavelength of C = 330/6 = 55 m.

image_prompt: SVG: radioactive decay scatter. x-axis Time / years (0 to 300, ticks every 20), y-axis Average percentage of radioactive nuclei remaining / % (0 to 100, ticks every 20). Noisy exponential decay from 100% at t=0 down to ~0% by t=300 (half-life ≈ 38 yr); teal × markers.

SVG: same radioactive decay data as the Q8 stem, shown as a point-to-point joined line. x-axis Time / years (0 to 300, ticks every 20), y-axis Average percentage of radioactive nuclei remaining / % (0 to 100, ticks every 20). Teal line through × markers, exponential decay from 100% to ~0%.

SVG: clean textbook illustration of a bar-tailed godwit wading bird in profile on grassland — long slightly upturned bill, speckled chestnut/brown plumage, pale supercilium stripe, standing on thin legs. Soft sky-to-grassland gradient, distinct palette from the IB photo.

SVG: four free-body force diagrams (Diagram 1-4) of a flying bird, each with Lift (up, green), Weight (down, brown), Drag (left, blue), Thrust (right, red) arrows. Diagram 1 = all forces balanced (correct for constant velocity B–C); Diagram 2 thrust>drag; Diagram 3 lift>weight; Diagram 4 drag>thrust and weight>lift. Coloured arrowheads, labels, panel titles.

SVG: infrasound waves diffracting over a hill at dusk. Source elephant on the right emits radiating wavefronts/arrows; curved wavefronts bend over the dark hill silhouette and reach a group of elephants and acacia trees on the far (left) side. Sunset gradient sky, black silhouettes, white wavefront arcs, dark direction arrows.

SVG: refraction of a light ray through three stacked transparent layers (top / middle / bottom). Ray travels upward, bending towards the dashed normal entering the denser middle layer and away from the normal entering the top layer. Dark backdrop, amber ray with arrowheads, dashed vertical normals, layer labels.

SVG: a blue plastic rod shown as a 3D cylinder carrying a net positive charge — several red '+' charges and a couple of remaining green '−' charges on its surface, with a colour key. Distinct palette from the IB original.

render_data.title: Charging a plastic rod by rubbing it with a cloth
render_data.substance: charged particles on the plastic rod surface
render_data.before: Before rubbing: the rod is electrically neutral — 6 positive charges (protons, fixed in the nuclei) and 6 negative charges (electrons), so net charge = 0.
render_data.after: After rubbing with the cloth: electrons are the only mobile charges, so the rod loses 2 electrons to the cloth. It is left with 6 positive and 4 negative charges, giving a net charge of +2 (positively charged).

SVG: strontium-90 radioactive-decay simulation panel.
